reasoning: While programmatic shingling recall is high (0.968), the structural fidelity score is extremely poor (0.27), indicating significant semantic and layout failures. The omission of the primary URL ('https://it.nc.gov/gicc') and the relocation of the header logo to the very end of the document (reading order 10) are material defects that violate accessibility and content integrity standards.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: The URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' is missing from the output paragraph that introduces the GICC Website. The paragraph element exists but is truncated after the colon, omitting the actual link which is the primary information of that section.

The second table (Committee Reports, element index 9) incorrectly identifies its first data row ('Statewide Mapping Advisory' and 'Paul Badr') as a header row ('is_header_row': true). In the source, this is clearly the first item in a list of reports, and no column headers exist for this section. Additionally, the first column contains bullet points that were merged into the text strings.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The list of reports starting with '• Statewide Mapping Advisory' and 'Paul Badr'.

concerns.analysis: The NCGICC logo, which appears at the very top of the source document as a primary branding element/header, is assigned a reading order of 10, placing it at the very end of the document after the committee reports. This is a material divergence from the intended top-to-bottom information flow.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: NCGICC logo at the top center of the page.

concerns.analysis: The URL 'https://it.nc.gov/gicc' which is explicitly written in the source document is missing from the output. The paragraph element intended to contain it only includes the label 'GICC Website: ', failing to provide the actual link or text for navigation.
concerns.location: Paragraph after 'Remaining 2021 GICC Quarterly Meeting Dates', element index 6

concerns.analysis: The document contains two clear list structures that are not preserved. First, the sub-list under Agenda item 4 (a, b, c) is flattened into a single block of text within a table cell. Second, the bulleted list of Committee Reports is converted into a table. While the table preserves the association between names, the semantic structural element of a list with markers is lost.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
a. Management and Operations Committee
b. Federal Interagency Committee
c. Technical Advisory Committee
concerns.output_quote:

action_items: Correct the reading order to ensure the NCGICC logo appears at the start of the document
Remediate the Committee Reports table to fix header identification and restore bulleted list semantics
